_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 7-15 Documentation/Patient Care Records (Revised 1/5/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan Procedures Protocol Section 7-15: Documentation/Patient Care Records — MDHHS Approved 1/5/2023
purpose: A Patient Care Report (PCR) is required for every EMS response. PCRs must be completed within 24 hours. PHI must be handled per Protected Health Information protocol. Time-sensitive presentations (stroke, MI, trauma) have specific additional documentation requirements.

sections.title: PCR Completion Requirements
points: A PCR is required on EVERY EMS response, including responses where the patient refuses care or transport
MCA selection determines the trigger: PCR is required when the unit 'is dispatched' OR 'arrives on scene' — per MCA configuration
PCR must be completed within 24 hours of the call
If addendum or correction is needed after initial submission: add addendum notation with date and time of correction; do not alter original documentation
PHI confidentiality: handle per Protected Health Information protocol

sections.title: Minimum Required PCR Elements
points: Patient demographics: name, date of birth, address, gender
Vital signs: at least ONE set for non-transported patients; at least TWO sets for transported patients
All medications administered with dose, route, and time
Patient disposition (transported, refused, DOA, transferred to other unit)

sections.title: Time-Sensitive Presentations — Additional Documentation
points: STROKE: document symptom onset time (last known well); Cincinnati/Los Angeles stroke scale results; destination facility
MYOCARDIAL INFARCTION / CHEST PAIN: document symptom onset time; attach 12-lead ECG to PCR; document STEMI findings if present
TRAUMA: document mechanism of injury; attach 12-lead ECG if obtained; document Glasgow Coma Scale; document vital signs with times

mca_selections_required.description: MCA must select when a PCR is required to be initiated/completed

deterioration_flags: Addendum needed after initial PCR submission — add addendum with current date/time; never alter original documentation
scope_notes: PCR required on every EMS response regardless of patient contact or transport
Complete within 24 hours; addendum if correction needed — never alter original
Transported patients: minimum 2 sets of vital signs
Time-sensitive presentations: symptom onset time, 12-lead attachment, stroke scale, MOI as applicable
PHI handling: per Protected Health Information protocol
